title: ISic001484. Epitaph of Philistidas, son of Euxenos
body:
Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy Prag 2016-10-10
1Φιλιστίδας
2Εὐξένο̄ hο
3[κ]α̣λ̣ο̄ποιό<ς>

Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, Jonathan Prag; edition source: autopsy Prag 2016-10-10
1ΦΙΛΙΣΤΙΔΑΣ
2ΕΥΞΕΝΟ̄HΟ
3[.]..Ο̄ΠΟΙΟ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text from autopsy;
  • 1: Trace of the cross bar of the Τ is visible
  • 3: Dubois: [κ]αλο̄ποιό(ς)

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Philistidas, son of Euxenos, the ropemaker

commentary

An unusual example of an archaic funerary inscription with indication of the deceased's profession: the restoration of initial Κ and interpretation of καλωποιός as 'rope-maker' is due to Orsi. The stone was presumed lost by Dubois (and speculatively attributed to Palermo Museum in LSAG(2), but seems always to have been in the Syracuse museum since its deposit in 1900.

bibliography

  • Orsi NSA 1900 281-282 fig.4 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001392
  • Hoffmann 1904 5216 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000817
  • Roehl 1907 Tab.34 no.12
  • Jeffery 1961 278 no.54 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000677
  • Dubois 1989 130 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000187
  • Jeffery Johnston 1990 278 no.54 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000678

Deeply cut letters of a regular, fairly square module, without serifs. Alpha with straight bar, standard delta, straight epsilon with equal bars, heta rectangular closed top and bottom, standard lambda, nu slants to the right, xsi in form of X, omicron of standard size, pi with shorter right hasta, four bar sigma with equal open strokes, phi with large eye and vetical stroke of equal size not over-extended.

Line 1 21-24 Line 2 20-22 Line 3 20-21 Interlineation line 1 to 2 10-13 Interlineation line 2 to 3 8
Licensed under a Creative Commons-Attribution 4.0 licence.
